title: nonatelia capitata
intro: Nonatelia capitata, also known as the "daisy of the forest," is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Nonatelia capitata include its ability to aid in digestion, reduce fever, and soothe respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
